title: potentilla arenaria subvar. minutula
intro: Potentilla arenaria subvar. minutula, also known as the dwarf potentilla or little five-spotted cinquefoil, is a small, perennial herb in the rose family (Rosaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, thus protecting against cell damage and promoting overall well-being. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
